items.claim: Anthropic committed to spend $100B+ on AWS over 10 years; Amazon investing $5B now with up to $25B additional (April 20, 2026)
items.notes: Anthropic pledges $100B+ AWS spend over 10 years incl. Trainium chips; secured 5GW capacity. Amazon invests $5B now, up to $25B additional on top of prior $8B.

items.claim: Anthropic enterprise customers spending $1M+/year doubled from 500 to 1,000+ in <2 months post-Series G close (April 2026)
items.notes: Anthropic disclosed enterprise customers spending $1M+/year doubled from 500 to 1,000+ in fewer than 2 months since Series G close (Feb 12, 2026).

items.claim: Anthropic IPO target as early as October 2026; Bloomberg reports unsolicited investor offers at $800B+ valuation
items.notes: Multiple reports indicate Anthropic evaluating IPO as early as Oct 2026. Bloomberg: unsolicited offers at $800B+ (>2x Series G $380B post-money).

items.claim: OpenAI confirmed $2B/month revenue (~$24B ARR) at $122B funding close (March 31, 2026)
items.notes: OpenAI stated $2B/month revenue (~$24B ARR) at time of $122B funding announcement. Full-year 2025 revenue cited as $13.1B.

items.claim: Microsoft launched M365 E7 enterprise plan at $99/user/month including Copilot (March 2026), 65% above prior top tier
items.notes: Microsoft M365 E7 at $99/user/month with Copilot bundled. 65% price premium vs prior top tier. Framed as offsetting $72B capex over prior 2 quarters.

items.claim: AWS AI services revenue run-rate estimated ~$15B (Q1 2026), driven by Bedrock multi-model strategy hosting both OpenAI and Anthropic
items.notes: AWS AI run-rate est. $15B Q1 2026. Multi-model Bedrock strategy (OpenAI + Anthropic) cited as key driver. This is analyst estimate, not official AWS figure.
items.parked_note: this is AWS not open AI

items.claim: HUMAIN $10B VC fund and UAE's Stargate AI infrastructure cluster represent sovereign-backed AI initiatives structured as infrastructure partnerships rather than direct majority investments into US AI labs.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: Formula Bot, built entirely using Bubble with no coding ability, generated $30,000 in its first three months after exploding to 100,000 visitors overnight from a Reddit post.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: Anthropic reached a landmark $1.5B settlement with authors, deleting works and shifting to legally acquired books.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: $3B has been invested into humanoids in 2025, up from $1.4B last year.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: Humanoid robotics investment grew from $1.4B in 2024 to $3B in 2025, contradicting predictions that investment would trail off.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: AI-first companies crossed tens of billions in real revenue at scale, with flagship labs stretching their lead with better capability-to-cost curves.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: NVIDIA ripped past $4T market capitalization and holds 90% ownership presence in AI research papers.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: NVIDIA holds approximately 90% ownership/presence in AI research papers, reflecting dominant market position that competitors have failed to meaningfully dent.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025
items.metricKey: share of AI research paper mentions/usage

items.claim: External safety organizations operate on annual budgets smaller than what leading AI labs collectively spend in a single day.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025
items.metricKey: annual budget relative to lab daily spend

items.claim: Cyber capabilities in AI doubled every 5 months, outpacing defensive measures, with criminals using AI agents to orchestrate ransomware infiltrating Fortune 500 companies.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: DeepSeek V3 is a 671B parameter MoE model that lowered training and inference cost using FP8 mixed precision, multi-token prediction, and auxiliary-free routing.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: Multi-GW (gigawatt) compute clusters moved from slideware to site plans, with power becoming the new bottleneck shaping AI roadmaps and margins.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: DeepSeek R1-lite-preview beat o1-preview on AIME 2024 pass@1 by scoring 52.5 vs. 44.6.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: OpenAI o1-preview scored 44.6 on AIME 2024 pass@1, lower than DeepSeek R1-lite-preview's 52.5.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: DeepSeek R1-Zero's AIME score rose from 15.6% to approximately 71% in roughly 8,500 training steps, with majority-vote runs reaching o1-0912 levels.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: DeepSeek R1 achieved AIME score of 79.8, MATH-500 score of 97.3, and GPQA score of 71.5 after full training pipeline including CoT warm start, SFT, and final RL pass.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning boosted performance on the Countdown task at 4K context to 83.4% (APR + RL) versus 60.0% for the baseline.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: RL reasoning approaches show minimal real gains and overfit easily, with many RL methods dropping 6-17% from reported results under standardized evaluation with no statistically significant improvements over baselines.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: AIME 2024 benchmark only has 30 examples where one question shifts Pass@1 by 3+ percentage points, causing double-digit performance swings and raising questions about benchmark reliability.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: Irrelevant and distracting facts increase error rates in models like DeepSeek R1, Qwen, Llama, and Mistral by up to 7x.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025
items.metricKey: error rate multiplier from distracting inputs

items.claim: DeepSeek R1-distill generates 50% more tokens than needed in 42% of cases versus 28% for R1, showing distillation makes models significantly more prone to overthinking.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: Adding a single seemingly relevant clause to math problems can cut model performance by approximately 65%, suggesting models do template-matching rather than true algebraic reasoning.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025
items.metricKey: performance reduction from single clause addition

items.claim: XReasoning shows that prompt-forcing models to reason in the user's language lifts match rates to ~98% on hard sets but reduces accuracy by 9-13 points.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: CMU's SafeLM reduces attack success rates from approximately 38.8% to approximately 8.3% at the base-model stage using safety-first pretraining with a 100B-token recontextualized corpus.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: CoT-based monitors flagged approximately 99% of reward-hacking attempts in red-teaming evaluations of frontier models, with only 3 failures out of 21,000 completions.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: Using 100-250 example post-training improves language match in cross-language reasoning but the accuracy penalty remains.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025
items.metricKey: post-training example count for language adaptation

items.claim: The report surveyed 1,200 AI practitioners on their AI usage patterns as part of the largest open-access survey of its kind.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: GPT-4o caught 95% of reward hacks by reading the reasoning traces of stronger models such as o3-mini, versus only 60% when monitoring actions alone.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025
items.metricKey: reward hack detection rate via CoT monitoring

items.claim: CoT monitoring via actions alone catches only 60% of reward hacks compared to 95% via reasoning trace monitoring.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025
items.metricKey: reward hack detection rate via action monitoring

items.claim: Anthropic finds less than 20% of true reasoning cues are verbalized in chain-of-thought, with faithfulness dropping on harder tasks.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: The NaturalReasoning dataset contains 2.8 million questions mined from pre-training corpora across major scientific fields.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: NaturalReasoning elicits the longest median chain-of-thought (434 words) among open datasets.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: Distilling an 8B Llama on just 0.5–2M NaturalReasoning problems yields steeper accuracy gains than training on larger WebInstruct / OpenMathInstruct sets, cutting tokens and compute.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: Oxford's LILO method achieves higher final test accuracy in 3× fewer training steps by prioritizing questions with high variance of success.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: OpenAI's experimental reasoning model reached IMO gold under contest-style conditions, scoring approximately 35/42 and solving 5/6 problems.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: GPT-5 solved 12/12 problems at the coding Olympics (ICPC-style test), with 11 solved on the first try.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: Gödel-Prover achieved 57.6% Pass@32 on miniF2F, a +7.6 improvement over prior open-source state-of-the-art, and generated 29.7k new Lean proofs.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: RL with LoRA reduces trainable parameters from billions to millions, shrinking gradients and optimizer state by roughly 10–50×.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: An actively fine-tuned 3.8B Phi-3 model via SIFT can outperform a base 27B Gemma-2 model on complex tasks.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025
items.metricKey: model performance at smaller parameter count

items.claim: Local Mixtures of Experts (test-time model merging) runs up to ~100× faster than SIFT-style test-time training while approaching TTT accuracy on ~1B base models.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: Subspace Boosting achieves greater than 10% gains when merging up to 20 specialist models, versus traditional methods that typically degrade after 5-10 merged models.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025
items.metricKey: vision benchmark improvement from model merging

items.claim: The Muon optimizer requires 10-15% fewer tokens than AdamW at large batch sizes (128K-16M), expanding the compute-time Pareto frontier.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: The 10-15% token efficiency gain from Muon saves millions of dollars at scale, while telescoping muP eliminates prohibitive hyperparameter search costs.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: When tested fairly with proper tuning, even the best optimizers including Muon achieve only ~10% speedups over AdamW at scale, debunking 2x speedup claims.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: The loss layer consumes up to 90% of training memory in modern LLMs as vocabularies grow.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025
items.metricKey: loss layer share of training memory

items.claim: Cut Cross Entropy (CCE) achieves a 24× reduction in memory consumption, taking Gemma 2's loss computation from 24GB down to just 1MB, while running ~5% faster than existing methods.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: CCE runs approximately 5% faster than the best existing loss computation methods.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: GPT-family models have a finite memorization capacity of approximately 3.6 bits per parameter.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: MuonClip with QK-clip enabled 15.5 trillion tokens of pretraining for Kimi K2 without loss spikes.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: Moonshot AI built Kimi K2, a 1 trillion parameter MoE model with 32B active parameters, trained using MuonClip.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: Kimi K2 ranks as the #1 open text model on LMArena.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: OpenAI released gpt-oss-120b and gpt-oss-20b in August 2025, their first open models since GPT-2, using MoE design with 5.1B active parameters (of 120B) and 3.6B active parameters (of 20B).
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: OpenAI's gpt-oss models received 20k GitHub stars in weeks post-release, though community vibes were described as 'mid' due to poor generalisation.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: In early 2024, Chinese models made up just 10 to 30% of new fine-tuned models on Hugging Face.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025
items.metricKey: share of new fine-tuned models on Hugging Face

items.claim: Qwen alone now accounts for more than 40% of new monthly model derivatives on Hugging Face, surpassing Meta's Llama.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: OpenRLHF delivers speedups of 1.22x–1.68x versus state-of-the-art RL training frameworks.
items.notes: From State of AI Report 2025

items.claim: Open-Sora 2.0 achieved commercial-level video generation quality from a training run costing approximately $200,000, reporting parity with HunyuanVideo and Runway Gen-3 Alpha.

items.claim: Meta Platforms signed a deal with CoreWeave for $14.2 billion in AI processing capacity through December 2031.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: Meta Platforms signed an additional deal with CoreWeave for $21 billion in AI processing capacity through December 2032.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave's total revenue backlog will reach $87.8 billion after the new Meta deal.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: Meta Platforms will represent 40.1 percent of CoreWeave's total revenue backlog after the new deal.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: OpenAI inked a five-year deal with CoreWeave for $11.9 billion in capacity in March 2025, with an additional $10.5 billion added later, bringing the total to $22.4 billion.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: OpenAI represents approximately 25.5 percent of CoreWeave's extended revenue backlog.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: Meta Platforms and OpenAI together represent nearly two-thirds of CoreWeave's guaranteed revenue backlog.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: In 2024, Microsoft represented 62 percent of CoreWeave's $1.92 billion in sales.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave's total revenue in 2024 was $1.92 billion.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave's top three customers accounted for 77 percent of revenues in 2024.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave had $5.13 billion in sales in 2025, up 2.7X from 2024.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave had an operating loss of $46 million in 2025.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave posted a net loss of $1.17 billion in 2025 due to stock compensation and other factors.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave has just shy of $4 billion in cash and marketable securities.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave spent $14.9 billion on capital equipment expenses in 2025, building out its datacenter fleet to 43 centers.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave ended 2025 with 850 megawatts of active power across its datacenter fleet.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave is estimated to have approximately 600,000 GPUs, mostly Nvidia H100s and H200s with a rising number of B200s and B300s.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave has 3.5 gigawatts of total power under contract.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave's GPU fleet is estimated to be worth between $15 billion and $20 billion before significant depreciation.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave has 2.25 gigawatts of unused contracted power to potentially build out.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: At Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's cited figure of $50 billion per gigawatt, CoreWeave would need approximately $113 billion to build out its unused contracted power capacity.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: Nvidia's cited figure for datacenter buildout cost is approximately $50 billion per gigawatt of capacity.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave announced a private offering of $1.75 billion in senior notes due in 2031.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave announced a $3 billion offering of senior convertible notes due in 2031.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave planned capital expenditures of $6–7 billion for early 2026, with full-year 2026 investment expected between $30–35 billion.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave went public in March 2025.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave had approximately 43 datacenters at end of 2025.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: Neocloud rental pricing is estimated at roughly half the rate of major hyperscalers, which charge 4X–5X the buildout cost over time.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points
items.metricKey: rental pricing relative to buildout cost

items.claim: CoreWeave has approximately two dozen named customers.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: CoreWeave's revenue grew 2.7X year-over-year from 2024 to 2025.
items.notes: From Coreweave data points

items.claim: Elon Musk is floating a 100 times revenue multiple valuation for Anthropic, implying a $3 trillion IPO valuation.
items.notes: From Platformonomics — Hyperscaler CapEx Tracker

items.claim: Approximately 40% of data center builds are reported to be behind schedule.
items.notes: From Platformonomics — Hyperscaler CapEx Tracker

items.claim: Hyperscaler CapEx spend is described as being in the hundreds of billions of dollars.
items.notes: From Platformonomics — Hyperscaler CapEx Tracker

items.claim: Oracle beat earnings estimates for Q3 FY26, causing Wall Street to send the stock upward.
items.notes: From Platformonomics — Hyperscaler CapEx Tracker

items.claim: AWS GPU on-demand pricing rose in April 2026: p5en.48xlarge increased from $36.18 to $41.61/hr; p5e in US West (N. California) reached $49.75/hr
items.notes: Spot/on-demand AWS H100/H200 instance prices rose in April 2026, signaling supply tightness as inference workloads scale.

items.claim: Eclipse VC closed a $1.3B fund focused on physical AI, robotics, and AI infrastructure startups (April 7, 2026)
items.notes: Eclipse (Cerebras backer) raised dedicated $1.3B fund for physical AI / robotics infrastructure investments.

items.claim: Forecaster Peter Wildeford projects combined OpenAI + Anthropic run rate could reach $240B by end of 2026
items.notes: Superforecaster projection following Anthropic $30B ARR milestone. Implies ~4x growth from current combined ~$55B run-rate.

items.claim: Chinese AI providers (Xiaomi, Alibaba, MiniMax, Zhipu, DeepSeek, StepFun) collectively exceeded 45% of OpenRouter weekly token volume in April 2026, up from <2% one year prior
items.notes: Combined Chinese model token share on OpenRouter passed 45% in April 2026; one year prior they were <2% of platform tokens.

items.claim: OpenAI's GPT-5.4 fell to #7 on OpenRouter rankings in April 2026; four of top five models are from Chinese providers
items.notes: GPT-5.4 dropped to #7 on OpenRouter token-volume rankings — a year prior GPT-4.1 dominated the chart.

items.claim: LLM API token prices fell approximately 80% across the industry from 2025 to 2026; Claude Opus per-input-token pricing dropped 67% (from $15 to $5/M)
items.notes: Industry-wide LLM API price compression continues — ~80% reduction year-over-year, putting pressure on AI provider unit economics.

items.claim: Google removed Gemini Pro models from the free tier as of April 1, 2026; Flash models remain free but with reduced daily quotas
items.notes: Google Gemini monetization shift: Pro tiers no longer free, Flash quotas reduced — signals push toward paid revenue.

items.claim: April 2026 saw 1,314 distinct startup funding events globally; 764 (~58%) involved AI or machine learning companies
items.notes: April 2026 single-month deal count: 1,314 events / 764 AI-related — concentration of capital in AI continues post-Q1 record.

items.claim: Defense AI funding reached $12.7B in April 2026, with Shield AI projecting $540M+ in 2026 revenue (80%+ YoY growth)
items.notes: Defense AI funding cohort hit $12.7B in April 2026; Shield AI projects $540M+ 2026 revenue at 80%+ growth.

items.claim: ServiceNow raised Now Assist 2026 AI ACV target to $1.5B (up from prior $1B target) following Q1 2026 earnings (April 22, 2026)
items.notes: ServiceNow raised 2026 Now Assist ACV target to $1.5B from $1B at Q1 2026 earnings — material lift in AI-specific commit guidance.

items.claim: ServiceNow Q1 2026 subscription revenue was $3,671M, up 22% YoY (19% in constant currency)
items.notes: ServiceNow Q1 2026 subscription revenue $3.671B (+22% YoY, +19% CC) — baseline for Now Assist attach analysis.

items.claim: ServiceNow Now Assist customers spending >$1M annual contract value grew over 130% year-over-year in Q1 2026
items.notes: $1M+ ACV Now Assist customer cohort grew 130%+ YoY in Q1 2026 — supports management's $1.5B raised guidance.

items.claim: Amazon guided for ~$200B in 2026 capex (much for AI infrastructure) and Alphabet plans ~$175B 2026 capex
items.notes: AMZN ~$200B and GOOGL ~$175B 2026 capex guidance — tracks against the $443B 2025 hyperscaler capex baseline.

items.claim: NVIDIA B300 spot pricing reached $2.45/GPU-hr on April 16, 2026 via Spheron — undercutting H200 rates; B200 spot hit $2.25/GPU-hr on April 18, 2026
items.notes: Blackwell spot rates on neoclouds (B300 $2.45/hr, B200 $2.25/hr in April 2026) now undercut older H200 rates — accelerating per-token cost compression.
